meta.name: IUS-12 Intolerance of Uncertainty Scale Short Form
meta.description: The IUS-12 is a 12-item short form of the Intolerance of Uncertainty Scale that measures reactions to ambiguous situations, uncertainty, and future events. It assesses two factors: Prospective Anxiety (desire for predictability) and Inhibitory Anxiety (paralysis of action due to uncertainty).
meta.author: Carleton, Norton & Asmundson (2007)
meta.reference: Carleton RN, Norton MA, Asmundson GJ. Fearing the unknown: A short version of the Intolerance of Uncertainty Scale. J Anxiety Disord. 2007;21(1):105-117.
meta.instruction: Please select the answer that best corresponds to how much each statement is characteristic of you.

commonChoices.label: Not at all characteristic of me

questions.text: It frustrates me not having all the information I need.
Uncertainty keeps me from living a full life.
One should always look ahead so as to avoid surprises.
A small unforeseen event can spoil everything, even with the best of planning.
When it's time to act, uncertainty paralyzes me.
When I am uncertain, I can't function very well.
I always want to know what the future has in store for me.
I can't stand being taken by surprise.
The smallest doubt can stop me from acting.
I should be able to organize everything in advance.
I must get away from all uncertain situations.

ranges.label: Low Intolerance of Uncertainty
ranges.description: Your intolerance of uncertainty is low. You appear to cope well with ambiguous and unpredictable situations.
suggestions: Continue leveraging your flexibility in uncertain situations
Use your adaptability as a strength in challenging circumstances

ranges.label: Moderate Intolerance of Uncertainty
ranges.description: You have a moderate level of intolerance of uncertainty. Some ambiguous situations may cause you discomfort or worry.
suggestions: Practice accepting uncertainty as a normal part of life
Challenge catastrophic thinking about unknown outcomes
Try gradual exposure to uncertain situations

ranges.label: High Intolerance of Uncertainty
ranges.description: Your intolerance of uncertainty is high. Ambiguity and unpredictability likely cause you significant distress and may interfere with daily functioning.
suggestions: Consider cognitive-behavioral strategies to address uncertainty-related anxiety
Practice mindfulness to stay grounded in the present moment
Work on tolerating small uncertainties and gradually increasing exposure
Consult a mental health professional for structured support

ranges.label: Very High Intolerance of Uncertainty
ranges.description: Your intolerance of uncertainty is very high. This level of discomfort with uncertainty may significantly impair your daily life and well-being.
suggestions: Strongly consider seeking professional help from a therapist experienced in anxiety disorders
Explore evidence-based treatments such as CBT or metacognitive therapy
Avoid excessive reassurance-seeking, which can maintain the cycle

prospective.label: Low
prospective.description: Low prospective anxiety. You handle anticipation of future events well.

prospective.label: Moderate
prospective.description: Moderate prospective anxiety. You sometimes worry about future uncertainties.

prospective.label: High
prospective.description: High prospective anxiety. The unpredictability of the future causes you significant concern.

prospective.label: Very High
prospective.description: Very high prospective anxiety. You experience intense distress about future uncertainties.

inhibitory.label: Low
inhibitory.description: Low inhibitory anxiety. Uncertainty does not significantly hinder your actions.

inhibitory.label: Moderate
inhibitory.description: Moderate inhibitory anxiety. Uncertainty sometimes holds you back from taking action.

inhibitory.label: High
inhibitory.description: High inhibitory anxiety. Uncertainty frequently paralyzes your decision-making and actions.

inhibitory.label: Very High
inhibitory.description: Very high inhibitory anxiety. Uncertainty severely impairs your ability to act and make decisions.
